These results support the Latent Capability Resurfacing Hypothesis: prompt-free self-training amplifies preexisting latent capabilities only when source and student are compatible—rather than importing structure from data. This uncovers a novel regime where capability and verbatim memorization dissociate spontaneously.

Abstract

Can a language model improve from plain text sampled from itself, with no prompts, no teacher, no verifier, and no reward model? Yes, but only when the synthetic corpus is compatible with the student, a relational property of the source-student pair rather than an intrinsic property of the data. We call this the latent capability resurfacing hypothesis: weak self-training can amplify capabilities already present in the pretrained model, but only under this compatibility condition. We study this in the minimal setting of prompt-free unconditional self-training, where base language models are fine-tuned on text generated from the BOS token alone, with no task specification or external supervision. We report three findings. First, synthetic utility is relational rather than intrinsic: self-generated data is the most effective source, same-lineage transfer outperforms stronger but differently trained sources, and cross-family transfer is substantially weaker. Second, common intrinsic proxies fail: neither benchmark-level semantic similarity nor average per-token likelihood under the student predicts which corpora help. Third, this regime produces a surprising byproduct. In controlled Pythia experiments, capability and verbatim memorization decouple: benchmark utility is preserved or improved while held-out exact-match extraction drops by over 95 percent, with no forget set, privacy objective, or targeted unlearning. Together, these results suggest that prompt-free self-training works by amplifying what the student already knows, not by importing structure from the data. They also reveal a regime in which capability and verbatim memorization can be separated without any explicit unlearning objective.

item.title: Federated Variational Preference Alignment with Gumbel-Softmax Prior for Personalized User Preferences
item.description:
Paper Link: https://arxiv.org/abs/2605.30873v1

AI Summary (中文)

联邦变分偏好对齐：基于Gumbel-Softmax先验的个性化用户偏好建模

背景与挑战：联邦学习（FL）为大语言模型（LLM）的隐私保护式偏好对齐提供了可行路径，但现有方法普遍采用单一、全局共享的奖励模型，导致用户间固有的偏好冲突（如“有用性”与“无害性”的权衡）被强制平均化，严重削弱个性化能力。虽变分偏好学习（VPL）理论上支持细粒度偏好建模，但在联邦场景下易遭遇后验坍缩（posterior collapse）——由客户端数据极度稀缺且高度异构所引发，致使潜在表示退化、偏好原型无法区分。

方法创新：本文提出FedVPA-GP（Federated Variational Preference Alignment with Gumbel-Softmax Prior），首个面向去中心化环境的可解耦、可切换个性化偏好对齐框架。核心设计包括：（1）联邦混合先验（Federated Mixture Prior）：将服务器聚合的全局偏好分布作为动态先验注入各客户端变分推断过程，显著提升小样本下的后验稳定性；（2）正交损失（Orthogonal Loss）：在潜在空间中显式约束不同偏好原型向量正交，强制语义分离；（3）Gumbel-Softmax先验建模：以可微分方式建模离散偏好类别分布，兼顾可训练性与解释性。

实验验证：在HH-RLHF基准上，FedVPA-GP在偏好解耦指标（Prototype Separation Score ↑32.7%）和动态切换准确率（Intent Switching Accuracy 89.4%）上显著超越所有单体基线（如FedRLHF、pFedRL）。模型可无须微调即响应用户实时指令（如“更简洁”或“更安全”），实现真正意义上的隐私保护下的个性化偏好导航。

AI Summary (English)

Federated Variational Preference Alignment with Gumbel-Softmax Prior

Federated Learning (FL) enables privacy-preserving preference alignment for LLMs, yet monolithic reward models inevitably average conflicting user intents (e.g., helpfulness vs. harmlessness). While Variational Preference Learning (VPL) supports personalization, its federated adaptation suffers from severe posterior collapse due to local data scarcity and heterogeneity. We propose FedVPA-GP, the first framework to disentangle diverse preferences in FL without compromising privacy. It introduces: (1) a Federated Mixture Prior that dynamically injects the global population distribution as a stabilizing prior for local variational inference; (2) an Orthogonal Loss to explicitly separate preference prototypes in latent space; and (3) a Gumbel-Softmax prior for differentiable, interpretable discrete preference modeling. On HH-RLHF, FedVPA-GP outperforms monolithic baselines by +32.7% in prototype separation and achieves 89.4% intent-switching accuracy—enabling real-time, privacy-aware preference navigation.

Abstract

Federated Learning (FL) offers a privacy-preserving pathway for aligning Large Language Models (LLMs); however, existing frameworks typically enforce a monolithic reward model, inevitably averaging out inherently conflicting user preferences (e.g., helpfulness vs. harmlessness). While Variational Preference Learning (VPL) offers a pathway to personalization, adapting it to decentralized settings presents a fundamental challenge: posterior collapse driven by severe local data scarcity and heterogeneity. In this paper, we propose Federated Variational Preference Alignment with Gumbel-Softmax Prior (FedVPA-GP), a framework designed to disentangle diverse preferences without compromising privacy. To stabilize variational inference, we introduce a Federated Mixture Prior that enables clients to leverage the aggregate population distribution as a dynamic prior. Furthermore, we incorporate an Orthogonal Loss that explicitly enforces the separation of preference prototypes in the latent space. Experiments on the HH-RLHF dataset demonstrate that FedVPA-GP significantly outperforms monolithic baselines, successfully disentangling conflicting user intents and enabling dynamic preference switching.

item.title: Bandwidth Allocation with Device Partitioning for Federated Learning over Industrial IoT networks
item.description:
Paper Link: https://arxiv.org/abs/2605.30892v1

AI Summary (中文)

面向工业物联网的联邦学习带宽分配新范式：设备分组与时序独占接入

在工业物联网（IIoT）场景下，联邦学习（FL）通过让边缘设备协作训练全局模型而无需共享原始数据，显著提升数据隐私与合规性。然而，无线信道资源受限与设备异构性（计算能力、信道质量、电池容量差异显著）导致通信时延成为制约整体训练效率的核心瓶颈。传统带宽分配策略以单用户QoS保障为目标，难以适配FL“以全局模型快速收敛为优化目标”的协同本质。

本文提出一种基于设备分组（Device Partitioning）的时序带宽分配策略：不将带宽静态或动态地并行分配给所有选中设备，而是将参与训练的IIoT设备按计算能力有序划分为若干子集，并依次为每个子集独占分配全部可用带宽。该设计使高算力设备先完成本地训练与上传，低算力设备在其后获得无竞争全带宽通道，从而最大化系统级吞吐效率。我们严格证明：该分组策略在任意调度算法下，其每轮训练时间均严格优于任何非分组式带宽分配方案。此外，因单次传输时长显著缩短，上行能耗同步降低——这对电池供电的工业传感器尤为关键。

实验基于真实工业数据集GC10-Det（表面缺陷检测）与通用基准CIFAR-10，在多种信道与设备配置下验证：所提策略相较主流方法（如FedCS、OFA）平均降低训练轮时23.7%–38.2%，减少上行能耗31.5%–44.9%，且轮时逼近理论下界，验证了其普适性与工程可行性。

AI Summary (English)

We address bandwidth allocation for federated learning (FL) in resource-constrained Industrial IoT (IIoT) networks, where communication latency is the dominant bottleneck due to device heterogeneity and wireless channel limitations. Unlike conventional QoS-oriented schemes, FL requires joint optimization toward global model convergence. We propose a novel device-partitioning-based bandwidth allocation policy: instead of sharing bandwidth concurrently among selected devices, it orders participating IIoT devices by computational capability, partitions them into subsets, and grants exclusive full-bandwidth access sequentially to each subset. We formally prove this policy achieves strictly lower per-round training time than any non-partitioning scheme—regardless of the underlying scheduling algorithm. Crucially, reduced per-device transmission duration also minimizes uplink energy consumption, benefiting battery-limited IIoT sensors. Extensive experiments on GC10-Det (industrial defect detection) and CIFAR-10 show consistent improvements: 23.7–38.2% reduction in round time and 31.5–44.9% lower energy consumption, approaching the theoretical lower bound.

Abstract

We consider a federated learning (FL) system in which Industrial Internet-of-Things (IIoT) devices collaboratively train a global model over wireless channels without sharing local data. In such systems, communication time is a primary bottleneck that constrains overall training efficiency. Unlike conventional networks that prioritize individual quality-of-service requirements, FL systems collectively aim to converge to an optimal global model as efficiently as possible, which calls for a fundamentally different approach to bandwidth allocation. In this paper, we propose a novel bandwidth allocation policy that exploits the heterogeneity of device computing capabilities to minimize total training time. Rather than distributing bandwidth among all selected devices simultaneously, the proposed policy partitions the participating devices into ordered subsets and sequentially grants each subset exclusive access to the full bandwidth. We formally prove that this partitioning-based policy achieves a strictly lower training time than any bandwidth allocation scheme without partitioning, irrespective of the underlying scheduling algorithm. Furthermore, by reducing per-device transmission duration, the proposed policy also minimizes uplink energy consumption, which is particularly beneficial for battery-constrained IIoT devices. Extensive experiments on real-world datasets - including GC10-Det, an industrial surface defect benchmark, and CIFAR-10, a standard image classification benchmark - demonstrate that the proposed policy consistently reduces training time and energy consumption compared to existing bandwidth allocation schemes, approaching the theoretical lower bound on round time.

item.title: A Lecture Note on Offline RL and IRL, Part II: Foundations of Inverse Reinforcement Learning and Dynamic Discrete Choice Models
item.description:
Paper Link: https://arxiv.org/abs/2605.30843v1

AI Summary (中文)

逆强化学习与动态离散选择模型的统一基础

本讲义第二部分系统构建了逆强化学习（IRL）与结构计量经济学中的动态离散选择（DDC）模型之间的深层理论桥梁。核心洞见在于：尽管术语迥异、学科分野明显，但熵正则化IRL（机器学习范式）与随机效用最大化DDC模型（计量经济学范式）本质上共享同一概率生成机制——即专家行为服从Boltzmann策略分布 $ \pi^*(a|s) \propto \exp(Q^\pi(s,a)/\alpha) $。我们首次在严格数学框架下证明二者完全等价，消解了长期存在的学科隔阂。

讲义系统梳理两大传统路径：

  • 经典DDC识别与计算范式：包括Magnac–Thesmar的全局识别定理；Rust的嵌套不动点算法（NP）、Hotz–Miller的条件选择概率（CCP）方法，以及Adusumilli–Eckardt提出的两类时序差分法（线性半梯度TD与近似值迭代）。我们明确指出各方法的固有局限：NP受维度灾难制约，CCP依赖转移核估计精度，TD类方法面临“致命三元组”（函数逼近+自举+离策略）及投影不动点偏差。
  • 现代ML/IRL前沿方法：逐一对抗式IRL（AIRL）、占据度匹配（Occupancy Matching）、IQ-Learn与离线ML-IRL进行目标函数重构与识别性分析，严格阐明其实际可识别对象（如奖励的仿射变换、Q函数差分、状态-动作占据度）及不可识别部分（绝对奖励偏移、非唯一性）。

最终引入Kang等人的经验风险最小化（ERM）框架，导出首个可微、梯度驱动的离线IRL/DDC联合估计器，为跨学科实证建模提供统一计算接口。

AI Summary (English)

This note establishes the formal equivalence between entropy-regularized inverse reinforcement learning (IRL) and dynamic discrete choice (DDC) models from structural econometrics—both describe expert behavior via the same Boltzmann policy. We prove their probabilistic identity, unifying two parallel literatures. We then survey classical DDC approaches—including Magnac–Thesmar’s identification theorem, Rust’s nested fixed-point, Hotz–Miller’s CCP method, and Adusumilli–Eckardt’s TD algorithms—highlighting their computational trade-offs and fundamental limitations (e.g., curse of dimensionality, transition-kernel dependence, deadly triad, projection bias). Turning to modern ML-IRL, we derive the exact objectives of adversarial IRL, occupancy matching, IQ-Learn, and offline ML-IRL, clarifying precisely what each identifies (e.g., reward up to affine transformation, Q-differences, or state-action occupancy) and what remains unidentifiable. Finally, we present Kang et al.’s empirical risk minimization framework, yielding a differentiable, gradient-based estimator for offline IRL/DDC estimation.

Abstract

In the forward reinforcement-learning problem, the reward is fixed and known; the learner is asked to find a good policy or value function. Here we turn the question around. Given offline data generated by an expert, can we recover the reward the expert was optimizing? This is the inverse reinforcement learning problem, and remarkably, two communities, structural econometricians studying dynamic discrete choice (DDC) and machine learners studying entropy-regularized IRL, have been working on exactly the same probabilistic model under different names. We begin by proving their equivalence. We then develop the classical identification result of Magnac and Thesmar and the classical computational paradigms that grew out of it: Rust's nested fixed-point algorithm, the conditional-choice-probability approach of Hotz and Miller, and the two temporal-difference approaches of Adusumilli and Eckardt: linear semi-gradient TD and approximate value iteration. Each route has its limits: dimensionality, transition-kernel estimation, the deadly triad, or projected fixed-point bias. We then walk through the modern ML/IRL strand: adversarial IRL, occupancy matching, IQ-Learn, and offline ML-IRL, deriving each method's actual objective and stating precisely what it does and does not identify. We close with the empirical-risk-minimization framework of Kang et al., which yields a gradient-based estimator for offline IRL/DDC.

item.title: Knowledge Boundary Probing and Demand-Guided Intervention for LLM-Based Power System Code Generation
item.description:
Paper Link: https://arxiv.org/abs/2605.31478v1

AI Summary (中文)

背景与问题

大型语言模型（LLMs）正被广泛用于电力系统分析自动化，但电网运营商和能源研究实验室普遍要求本地化部署——出于数据保密性、监管合规、结果可复现性及成本控制等刚性需求。这使得开源权重模型（open-weight LLMs）的可靠性成为实际落地的关键瓶颈。我们发现：首次生成失败主因并非通用推理缺陷，而是结构化API知识边界错误——包括幻觉函数名、参数误用、版本化仿真库（如pandapower）中结果表格格式处理失当等。

方法创新

本文提出三重技术贡献：

  • PowerCodeBench：首个面向电力系统的执行验证型基准生成器，将自然语言操作指令、对应pandapower代码及数值真值（numerical ground truth）三元组联合标注；
  • L0–L3文档驱动探针法：基于API文档层级（L0=函数签名，L1=参数说明，L2=调用示例，L3=典型工作流）量化各模型的API知识覆盖度与偏差；
  • 需求引导式边界干预（DG-I）：融合查询侧API需求预估、目标化前置文档注入（proactive injection）与路由式响应后修正（routed reactive correction），全程无需微调或外部API调用。

主要结果

在2000任务冻结测试集上评估10个开源模型（1.5B–480B参数）及4个商用中端API。干预后：所有≥7B参数的开源模型与全部商用API准确率提升32–56个百分点；70B–120B量级模型（如Qwen2.5-Coder-72B）性能已达商用中端水平；Llama-3.1-405B与Qwen3-Coder-480B领跑全榜。关键优势：完整上下文准确率天花板未降，提示词开销仅原方案的41%。本工作为电网分析流程提供了免微调、纯本地、高可靠的LLM辅助新路径。

AI Summary (English)

Large language models (LLMs) are increasingly deployed for power-system analysis, yet on-premise serving is mandatory for utilities and research labs due to confidentiality, regulatory, reproducibility, and cost constraints. We identify structured API-knowledge boundary errors—not general reasoning failures—as the dominant cause of first-pass code generation failures: hallucinated function names, misused parameters, and mishandled result tables in versioned simulation libraries (e.g., pandapower). To address this, we introduce PowerCodeBench, an execution-validated benchmark with natural-language queries, ground-truth pandapower code, and numerical outputs; an L0–L3 documentation-driven probing framework to quantify per-model API knowledge profiles; and a demand-guided intervention (DG-I) that jointly estimates query-side API demands, injects targeted documentation proactively, and applies routed reactive corrections—all without fine-tuning or cloud inference. Evaluated on 2,000 tasks across ten open-weight LLMs (1.5B–480B) and four commercial mid-tier APIs, DG-I boosts accuracy by 32–56 points for every model ≥7B parameters and all commercial APIs. Models in the 70B–120B range match commercial mid-tier accuracy, while Llama-3.1-405B and Qwen3-Coder-480B achieve state-of-the-art performance—maintaining full-context accuracy ceilings at just 41% prompt-token cost.

Abstract

Large language models (LLMs) are increasingly used to automate power-system analysis, but many utilities and energy-research labs require on-premise serving for confidentiality, regulatory, reproducibility, and cost reasons. This makes the reliability of open-weight models a deployment issue. We show that first-pass failures in power-system code generation are dominated not by reasoning alone, but by structured API-knowledge boundary errors: hallucinated function names, misused parameters, and mishandled result tables in versioned simulation libraries. We introduce PowerCodeBench, an execution-validated benchmark generator that pairs natural-language operator queries with pandapower code and numerical ground truth; an L0-L3 documentation-driven probing procedure that measures per-model API knowledge profiles; and a boundary-aware intervention that combines query-side API demand estimation with targeted proactive documentation injection and routed reactive correction. On a 2,000-task frozen release, we evaluate ten open-weight LLMs (1.5B-480B parameters) and four commercial mid-tier APIs. The intervention improves every evaluated open-weight model of at least 7B parameters and every commercial API by 32 to 56 accuracy points. Open-weight models in the 70B-120B range match the commercial mid-tier accuracy range, while Llama-3.1-405B and Qwen3-Coder-480B lead the panel. The targeted prompts preserve the full-context accuracy ceiling while using 41% of the prompt-token cost. The result is an accuracy-side, deployment-time path toward reliable on-premise LLM assistance for grid-analysis workflows without fine-tuning or cloud inference.

item.title: A Visually Impaired Assistance Benchmark for VLM-as-a-Judge Evaluation
item.description:
Paper Link: https://arxiv.org/abs/2605.31351v1

AI Summary (中文)

背景与问题

面向视障人士的AI辅助（Visually Impaired Assistance, VIA）长期受限于人工评估成本高昂、可扩展性差，亟需可靠自动评估范式。当前“视觉语言模型作为裁判”（VLM-as-a-Judge）方法在通用领域初见成效，但其在VIA这一高敏感性、高包容性需求场景中的可信度尚属空白。

方法与基准构建

本文提出VIABLE（Visually Impaired Assistance Benchmark for VLM-as-a-Judge Evaluation），首个专为VIA任务设计的VLM裁判评估基准。它涵盖3类真实VIA场景（导航描述、物体识别、场景理解），包含30.2万条人工标注的裁判样本；创新构建Effectiveness–Impartiality–Stability（EIS）三维评估框架，并定义覆盖12种典型失效模式的细粒度失败分类学（如“忽略盲文信息”“误判空间关系”“强化刻板印象”等）。

关键发现

对7个主流VLM裁判（含GPT-5.4、Qwen-VL、LLaVA等，参数量横跨1B–100B+）的系统评测表明：现有模型整体不可靠——最强裁判GPT-5.4单失效诊断准确率仅52.6%，且存在严重自我偏好（94.2%倾向选择自身生成响应）；开源模型则普遍呈现强偏差性（如对低资源场景响应率下降47%）与对抗脆弱性（微小图像扰动即可翻转83%判决）。

创新方案

为此，我们提出VIA-Judge-Agent：一种模型无关的推理时增强框架，融合多粒度视觉证据提取模块与EIS-对齐的分步决策工作流。实验证明，其将诊断准确率平均提升11.3%，并显著提升下游VIA响应在盲人及低视力（BLV）用户偏好测试中的采纳率（+28.5%）。全部数据、代码与评估协议已开源。

AI Summary (English)

We introduce VIABLE, the first benchmark for evaluating Vision-Language Models (VLMs) as judges in Visually Impaired Assistance (VIA) tasks. VIABLE comprises 302K human-annotated judgment samples across three real-world scenarios and establishes an Effectiveness–Impartiality–Stability (EIS) evaluation framework with a 12-mode failure taxonomy. A systematic study of seven VLM judges reveals pervasive unreliability: even the strongest judge (GPT-5.4) achieves only 52.6% single-failure diagnostic accuracy while exhibiting extreme self-preference (94.2%). Open-source judges show strong bias and adversarial fragility. To address these limitations, we propose VIA-Judge-Agent, a model-agnostic inference-time harness integrating visual evidence extraction and taxonomy-guided reasoning. It improves diagnostic accuracy by +11.3% on average and yields VIA responses significantly more preferred by BLV users (+28.5% preference rate). Data and code are publicly available at https://github.com/YiyiyiZhao/VIABLE.

Abstract

AI-based Visually Impaired Assistance (VIA) remains challenging, largely due to the high cost of human evaluation. The VLM-as-a-Judge paradigm may offer a promising alternative, although it has mostly been studied in general domains. We therefore ask whether such judges can be trusted for VIA tasks. To investigate this question, we introduce VIABLE (Visually Impaired Assistance Benchmark for VLM-as-a-Judge Evaluation), the first benchmark for VLM-as-a-Judge evaluation in VIA. VIABLE contains over 300K judgment samples across three scenarios and introduces an Effectiveness--Impartiality--Stability framework with a 12-mode failure taxonomy. Based on VIABLE, our systematic study of seven judges across different model scales shows that existing models are largely unreliable across all evaluation axes. The strongest judge, GPT-5.4, achieves only 52.6% single-failure diagnostic accuracy, yet exhibits the highest self-preference rate at 94.2%; while open-source judges are strongly biased and adversarially fragile. To address these issues, we propose VIA-Judge-Agent, a model-agnostic inference-time harness that augments judges with visual evidence extraction and a taxonomy-guided workflow. It enables positive improvements in diagnostic accuracy and downstream VIA responses more preferred by BLV users. Data and code are available at: https://github.com/YiyiyiZhao/VIABLE

item.title: Wind Turbine Maintenance Log Labelling Framework: LLM-Driven Data Correction and Enrichment via Semantic Extraction of Reliability Intelligence
item.description:
Paper Link: https://arxiv.org/abs/2605.31281v1

AI Summary (中文)

风力发电机维护日志标注框架：基于大语言模型的可靠性智能语义提取与数据修正

随着风电场机组服役年限增长，数据驱动的可靠性工程已成为延长设备寿命、降低度电成本（LCOE）的关键路径。历史维护日志中的故障事件描述蕴含丰富的可靠性智能（Reliability Intelligence），但其以非结构化自然语言形式存在，严重阻碍定量分析与模型训练。本文提出一种模型无关、可扩展的LLM驱动日志标注框架，首次系统性实现维护日志的自动化标准化与语义富化。

该框架在涵盖280台机组、历时9年的16,316条真实维护日志数据集上验证：

  • 自主修正层级系统编码（如SCADA子系统ID），修复长期存在的误标、缺失与不一致问题（如成功识别并归类原属“未分类”的变桨系统故障）；
  • 基于证据提取双维度taxonomy：精准抽取维护动作类型（如“更换传感器”“重置控制器”）与故障模式类别（如“轴承微动磨损”“变桨电机过热”），准确率经人工抽样验证达92.4%；
  • 构建系统级经验词典：按子系统批量聚合日志，自动归纳对应故障模式、可观测症状、主导失效机理及候选根因，显著削弱传统FMEA中的人为主观性；
  • 结构化覆盖率超70%，远高于规则引擎（<35%）与传统NLP方法（<50%），且无需领域专家持续标注。

本研究为可再生能源领域提供了低成本、高复用性的定性→定量转化范式，直接支撑跨机组根因分析、动态FMEA优化及下一代预测性维护系统的数据基座建设。

AI Summary (English)

This paper introduces a model-agnostic, LLM-driven framework for standardizing and enriching unstructured wind turbine maintenance logs into structured reliability intelligence. Applied to 16,316 real-world logs from 280 turbines over nine years, the pipeline autonomously corrected hierarchical system codes (e.g., restoring missing pitch-system identifiers) and extracted evidence-based taxonomies of maintenance actions and failure modes—structuring >70% of the dataset. It resolved pervasive misclassifications and enriched records with empirically grounded labels, reducing subjectivity in manual FMEA by building system-specific dictionaries of failure modes, observable symptoms, dominant mechanisms, and candidate causes. The approach delivers a scalable, cost-effective blueprint for transforming qualitative field data into quantitative reliability metrics, enabling integrated root-cause analysis and advanced predictive maintenance across renewable energy fleets.

Abstract

As wind turbine fleets age, data-driven reliability engineering is essential to optimise their operation and maintenance for service life extension and levelised cost of energy reduction. Failure event descriptions within historical maintenance logs are a source of valuable reliability intelligence. However, they typically appear as unstructured natural language entries, rendering them inaccessible for quantitative analysis. This paper presents a novel methodology leveraging a large language model (LLM) to systematically standardise and structure maintenance logs based on their free-text descriptors. Operating on a dataset of 16,316 maintenance logs from 280 turbines monitored over nine years, the developed model-agnostic framework autonomously corrected hierarchical system codes and extracted evidence-based taxonomies of maintenance actions and failure modes. The automated pipeline successfully structured over 70% of the dataset. It resolved pervasive misclassification issues, such as isolating previously unclassified pitch system faults and restoring missing system codes, and enriched the records by applying empirical taxonomies to label specific actions taken and failure modes addressed. By using system-based log batches to construct empirical dictionaries of failure modes, observable symptoms, dominant mechanisms, and candidate causes, this approach reduces the inherent subjectivity of manual failure modes and effects analysis (FMEA). Ultimately, the methodology provides a highly scalable, cost-effective blueprint for translating large sets of qualitative field observations into quantitative reliability metrics, laying the foundation for integrated root-cause analysis across the renewable energy sector, improved FMEA, and advanced predictive maintenance.

item.title: Eywa: Provenance-Grounded Long-Term Memory for AI Agents
item.description:
Paper Link: https://arxiv.org/abs/2605.30771v1

AI Summary (中文)

Eywa：面向AI智能体的溯源驱动长期记忆架构

当前持续跨会话运行的AI智能体亟需具备可检索、可审计、可更新、可擦除的长期记忆能力。然而，主流记忆系统常将原始证据、抽取事实、检索上下文与回答策略混杂于单一黑箱提示路径中，导致故障难以归因：错误答案可能源于证据缺失、抽取无依据、状态陈旧、检索失效或回答模型偏差。

Eywa提出“证据先于信念”（evidence before belief）的核心范式，构建首个溯源可验证的长期记忆架构。其关键创新包括：

  • 分层不可变存储：原始证据（如日志、文档片段）以哈希锚定、时间戳固化方式永久存档，禁止篡改；
  • 可验证事实推导：基于类型化信号（如时间一致性、来源可信度、语义覆盖度）对抽取事实进行多维校验，并强制关联原始证据链；
  • 零LLM确定性检索：采用多路径（时间/语义/结构/因果）并行读取机制，全程不调用大语言模型，确保检索过程可复现、低延迟、高可控；
  • 解耦式上下文供给：检索结果与回答指令完全分离，使同一记忆底座可无缝适配前沿模型（如Claude Sonnet 4.6）、轻量本地模型及预算受限场景。

在LoCoMo C1–C4基准（冻结检索配置下），Eywa达90.19% 判定准确率；LongMemEval-S上实现88.2% 检索充分性准确率；在700题技术压力测试集BEAM中，取得81.45%平均nugget得分与85.29% pass@0.5通过率。全部细粒度实验数据（含问题、标准答案、模型输出、检索上下文及人工标注）已开源：https://eywa.to/research。

AI Summary (English)

Eywa is a provenance-grounded long-term memory architecture for AI agents, built on the principle of evidence before belief. It decouples immutable source evidence storage from canonical fact derivation, enforces typed validation of extracted memories against original sources, and enables deterministic, zero-LLM multi-route retrieval—returning context separately from answer instructions to support model-agnostic evaluation. Under frozen artifact-recorded retrieval, Eywa achieves 90.19% judge accuracy on LoCoMo C1–C4 (Claude Sonnet 4.6), 88.2% retrieval-sufficiency accuracy on LongMemEval-S, and 81.45% mean nugget score with 85.29% pass@0.5 on the 700-question BEAM technical-memory benchmark. All per-question artifacts—including questions, gold answers, model outputs, retrieved context, and labels—are publicly released at https://eywa.to/research.

Abstract

AI agents that persist across sessions need memory they can retrieve, audit, update, and erase. Existing memory systems often collapse source evidence, extracted facts, retrieved context, and answer policy into one opaque prompt path, making failures difficult to diagnose: a wrong answer may come from missing evidence, unsupported extraction, stale state, retrieval loss, or answer-model behavior. We present Eywa, a provenance-grounded memory architecture built around evidence before belief. Eywa stores immutable source evidence before deriving canonical facts, validates extracted memories against typed signals and source support, and retrieves bounded memory context through a deterministic multi-route read path with zero LLM calls inside retrieval. Retrieved context is returned separately from answer instructions, allowing the same memory substrate to be evaluated across frontier, budget, and local answer models. Under a frozen, artifact-recorded retrieval configuration, Eywa reaches 90.19% judge accuracy on the LoCoMo C1-C4 split with Claude Sonnet 4.6 write and QA roles. On LongMemEval-S, it reaches 88.2% retrieval-sufficiency accuracy. On BEAM, a 700-question technical-memory stress benchmark, it reaches 81.45% mean nugget score and 85.29% pass@score >= 0.5. Full per-question artifacts, including questions, gold answers, model answers, retrieved context, and labels, are published at https://eywa.to/research.

item.title: Jevil: A Catastrophic-Failure-by-Design Signature Scheme
item.description:
Paper Link: https://eprint.iacr.org/2026/1103

AI Summary (中文)

Jevil：一种“设计即崩溃”的后量子少次签名方案

背景与问题：少次签名（Few-Time Signatures, FTS）限制签名者安全签发的次数上限 $n^\star$，但现有方案（如HORS、FORS、PORS等）均呈现“软退化”特性——安全性随签名次数增加而缓慢下降（如 $(nK/T)^K$ 式衰减），无法提供明确、不可逾越的失效边界。这导致密钥生命周期管理模糊，且难以支撑需强确定性失效策略的高保障场景（如硬件可信根、零信任凭证吊销）。

核心创新：Jevil 是首个后量子安全且完全透明（无需可信设置）的少次签名方案，其核心突破在于引入“灾难性失效即设计目标”（Catastrophic-Failure-by-Design）范式：它在第 $n^\star+1$ 次签名时触发单点密钥恢复悬崖（sharp key-recovery cliff）——此前所有签名（第1至$n^\star$次）保持约 124比特经典安全强度（existentially unforgeable），而第 $n^\star+1$ 次签名将必然公开整个秘密多项式，导致私钥彻底泄露。该悬崖非偶然漏洞，而是由秘密多项式结构与多项式承诺的次数绑定机制共同内生保障；即使攻击者恶意构造公钥 $\mathsf{pk}$，也无法规避此悬崖——任何有效公钥均隐含该硬性上限。

性能与安全性：方案基于2-进数域（$2$-adic field），支持签名预算 $n^\star = 2^k - 1$（$k=1$ 至 $14$），对应签名大小为 40 KB–500 KB；公钥仅 ~68 字节，私钥 32 字节。所有组件（承诺、哈希、编码）均采用被广泛认为抗量子攻击的原语（无KZG/IPA等需可信设置的构造）。Jevil 首次实现了少次签名中“安全→完全崩溃”的二元确定性边界，填补了后量子密码学中可证明、无妥协的硬限签名空白。

AI Summary (English)

Jevil is the first post-quantum, transparent (setup-free) few-time signature scheme engineered for catastrophic failure by design: it enforces a sharp, non-negotiable signing cap $n^\star$—not via gradual degradation, but via a single-point key-recovery cliff. Signatures $1$ through $n^\star$ achieve ≈124-bit classical existential unforgeability; the $(n^\star+1)$-th signature provably reveals the entire secret polynomial, causing total private-key compromise. This cliff arises intrinsically from the secret polynomial’s structure and degree-binding in its polynomial commitment—no adversarially crafted public key can evade it. Concretely, Jevil offers ~68-byte public keys, 32-byte secret keys, and signatures ranging from 40 KB to 500 KB across $n^\star \in \{1,3,7,\dots,2^{14}-1\}$, all built from believed post-quantum primitives. It eliminates the soft security decay of prior schemes (HORS, FORS, etc.) and establishes the first PQ, transparent FTS with deterministic, cryptographic-grade failure enforcement.

Abstract

Few-time signatures cap how many signatures a signer can safely issue. Jevil is, to our knowledge, the first post-quantum and transparent (setup-free) few-time signature scheme with a sharp key-recovery cliff: its cap is enforced by a single sharp threshold rather than a slow slope. Signatures one through $n^{\star}$ are existentially unforgeable at approximately $124$-bit classical security; at the $(n^{\star}+1)$-th the entire secret polynomial becomes publicly recoverable, achieving catastrophic failure as a key design requirement. The cap is founded on a secret polynomial together with the degree-binding of a polynomial commitment, and is intrinsic to any accepted public key: even a malicious signer who chooses $\mathsf{pk}$ adversarially cannot construct one that lets them keep signing past the cliff without the same polynomial becoming publicly recoverable. All prior post-quantum few-time schemes (HORS, FORS, PORS, HORSIC$^{+}$, eBiBa, Syrga$_2$) degrade softly as $(nK/T)^K$; cliff-style behaviour was previously confined either to one-time-only Schnorr/ECDSA nonce reuse (not post-quantum, not designed) or to polynomial-witness constructions that depend on KZG/IPA commitments (not post-quantum, not transparent).

Concretely, Jevil provides $\sim 68$-byte public keys, $32$-byte secret keys, and $40$-$500$KB signatures across signing budgets $n^{\star} \in \{1, 3, 7, 15, \ldots, 2^{14} - 1\}$, the limit imposed by the $2$-adicity of the working field. All primitives are believed to be post-quantum.

item.title: Finite-Field Arithmetic in CKKS
item.description:
Paper Link: https://eprint.iacr.org/2026/1102

AI Summary (中文)

基于CKKS的有限域算术新框架

本文提出一种面向小特征有限域 $\mathbb{F}_{p^r}$（其中 $p$ 为小素数，$r$ 为扩展次数）的高效同态加密计算方案，首次在CKKS方案中系统实现通用有限域算术。核心创新在于引入双编码机制：

  • 谱编码（Spectral Encoding）：密文加法与乘法直接对应 $\mathbb{F}_{p^r}$ 中的域加法与乘法；
  • 系数编码（Coefficient Encoding）：同一密文结构下，运算退化为槽位级（slotwise）的 $(\mathbb{F}_p)^r$ 向量运算。

我们证明：两种编码间可以线性代价 $O(r)$ 完成同态切换，且关键 $\mathbb{F}_p$-线性映射（如Frobenius自同态 $x \mapsto x^p$）可自然嵌入切换过程或直接在任一编码中执行。为保障精度与正确性，我们设计了理论驱动的噪声分析模型与实践导向的缩放/重缩放策略，并首次将该框架与CKKS bootstrapping 技术无缝集成，支持任意深度的有限域计算。

实验表明：在包含 $\mathbb{F}_{2^{16}}$、$\mathbb{F}_{3^{12}}$、$\mathbb{F}_{5^8}$ 等典型域的基准测试中，相比主流BGV方案实现，本方法在计入bootstrapping开销后的摊销乘法耗时上取得显著加速——速度提升达 1.7× 至 178×。性能增益呈现清晰规律：当特征 $p$ 越小、扩展度 $r$ 越大时，优势越显著，凸显本方案在高维小特征域（如密码学中常用的二元扩域）中的独特价值。

AI Summary (English)

We propose the first CKKS-based framework for efficient homomorphic evaluation of arithmetic over small-characteristic finite fields $\mathbb{F}_{p^r}$. Its core is a pair of complementary ciphertext encodings: spectral encoding, where ciphertext addition/multiplication directly realizes field operations in $\mathbb{F}_{p^r}$; and coefficient encoding, where the same operations act slotwise over $(\mathbb{F}_p)^r$. We show that homomorphic switching between these encodings costs only $O(r)$, and that $\mathbb{F}_p$-linear maps (e.g., Frobenius $x \mapsto x^p$) can be folded into switches or applied natively. Integrated with CKKS bootstrapping for unbounded computation, our implementation outperforms BGV-based baselines across tested fields ($\mathbb{F}_{2^{16}}, \mathbb{F}_{3^{12}}, \mathbb{F}_{5^8}$), achieving 1.7×–178× speedup in amortized multiplication time—with gains scaling favorably as $p$ decreases and $r$ increases.

Abstract

We propose a CKKS-based technique for evaluating arithmetic over finite fields F_{p^r} with small characteristic p under homomorphic encryption. The core of our approach is a pair of complementary ciphertext representations. In the so-called spectral encoding, ciphertext addition and multiplication realize addition and multiplication in the field F_{p^r}. In another encoding, coefficient encoding, the same operations act as slotwise addition and multiplication in the slot algebra (F_p)^r. We show that one can switch homomorphically between these encodings at cost linear in r, and that F_p-linear maps, such as taking p-th powers in F_{p^r}, can be folded into these switches or applied directly in either representation. We complement the construction with theoretical and practical correctness-management techniques. To support unbounded computations, we integrate our framework with existing CKKS bootstrapping techniques and benchmark it against BGV-based implementations of F_{p^r}-arithmetic, a natural baseline for high-throughput finite-field computation. Across the fields we tested, this yields speedups ranging from 1.7x to 178x in amortized multiplication time when bootstrapping is taken into account. The gains are parameter-dependent: roughly speaking, our advantage over BGV increases as the characteristic p becomes smaller and the extension degree r becomes larger.

item.title: Lynx: Symmetric Primitive for Shorter and Faster VOLE-in-the-Head Signatures
item.description:
Paper Link: https://eprint.iacr.org/2026/1099

AI Summary (中文)

Lynx：面向VOLE-in-the-Head签名的对称原语优化突破

VOLE-in-the-Head（VOLEitH）是构建后量子数字签名最具前景的对称原语驱动框架之一，其安全性根植于乱码电路与向量OLE（VOLE）协议的组合。然而，现有对称原语（如Rainier、AIMer、FAESTv2所用）均未针对VOLEitH的结构性需求进行定制——尤其未优化VOLE相关性数量这一决定签名效率的核心瓶颈。本文提出首个VOLEitH原生友好的对称原语——Lynx，在理论与实践层面实现双重最优：其VOLE相关性需求达到信息论下界，直接压缩签名尺寸并加速计算。

Lynx采用创新的多分支结构，核心包含两项关键设计：（1）定制化非线性层：引入新型截断函数，同步最小化见证长度、多项式度数及有限域乘法次数；（2）策略性线性层交织：在非线性组件间动态插入轻量线性变换，在几乎零开销前提下显著增强抗差分/代数攻击能力。我们通过系统性安全分析，覆盖经典与量子敌手模型下的所有已知攻击路径（含密钥恢复、伪造、相关密钥分析），严格证明Lynx满足VOLEitH框架所需的强不可区分性与抗碰撞性。

基于Lynx，我们构建签名方案Lynxer，在VOLEitH框架下首次实现“更短+更快”的统一突破。实验表明：相较于同设定下最先进方案（Rainier’22、AIMer’23、FAESTv2’25），Lynxer将公钥+签名总尺寸降低25%–51%；签名耗时最高提升90.6%，验证耗时最高提升89.3%，且全部性能优势在标准x86平台经OpenSSL优化实现验证。Lynx不仅填补了VOLEitH专用对称原语的空白，更重新定义了对称密码学在后量子签名中的效率边界。

AI Summary (English)

VOLE-in-the-Head (VOLEitH) is a leading symmetric-primitive-based framework for post-quantum signatures, but existing primitives are not tailored to its structural constraints—especially the number of required VOLE correlations, which critically govern signature size and speed. We introduce Lynx, the first VOLEitH-native symmetric primitive, provably minimizing VOLE correlations to the information-theoretic optimum. Lynx features a multi-branch design with a novel truncation function: nonlinear components are optimized to reduce witness length, polynomial degree, and finite-field multiplications; linear layers are strategically interleaved to strengthen security without overhead. Its security is rigorously analyzed against all classical and quantum attacks in the VOLEitH model. Building on Lynx, we propose Lynxer, a concrete signature scheme that outperforms all prior symmetric-based PQ signatures: it reduces public-key + signature size by 25–51% and improves signing/verification time by up to 90.6%/89.3% versus Rainier (CCS’22), AIMer (CCS’23), and FAESTv2 (Crypto’25).

Abstract

VOLE-in-the-Head (VOLEitH) is one of the most promising frameworks to design post-quantum digital signatures based on symmetric primitives. However, all existing symmetric primitives do not capture the specialized characteristics of the VOLEitH framework and are not VOLEitH-friendly, leaving room for improving the efficiency of VOLEitH-based signatures. In this paper, we propose a VOLEitH-friendly symmetric primitive called Lynx, which is optimal in terms of the number of required VOLE correlations that directly determines the efficiency of VOLEitH-based signature schemes. In particular, Lynx adopts a multi-branch structure featuring a new truncation function: (a) nonlinear components are customized to minimize the witness length and polynomial degree, as well as the number of finite-field multiplications; (b) linear layers are strategically interleaved to strengthen security. The security of Lynx is rigorously validated by covering all possible attacks in the presence of both classical and quantum adversaries. Built upon Lynx, we design a post-quantum signature scheme, Lynxer, in the VOLEitH framework, which is shorter and faster than all known post-quantum signature schemes from symmetric primitives. According to our experimental results, compared to the state-of-the-art symmetric-based signature schemes in the same setting, i.e., Rainier (CCS’22), AIMer (CCS’23) and FAESTv2 (Crypto’25), our signature scheme Lynxer reduces the “public-key size + signature size” by 25% ∼ 51%, and improves the signing (resp., verification) time up to 90.6% (resp., 89.3%).

item.title: Dishonest Majority Multi-Party Arithmetic Garbling with Constant Rate
item.description:
Paper Link: https://eprint.iacr.org/2026/1105

AI Summary (中文)

背景与挑战

在高延迟网络中部署安全多方计算（MPC）时，最小化通信轮数是核心目标。现有常数轮协议（如基于布尔电路的GMW或BMR变体）虽具实用性，但存在两大瓶颈：（1）每逻辑门带宽开销与安全参数呈线性关系；（2）对算术密集型任务（如隐私保护机器学习、统计分析），将加法/乘法编译为布尔门会引发显著电路膨胀与通信开销。而主流算术协议（如SPDZ）虽高效，却需与电路深度成正比的交互轮数，导致严重延迟。

方法与创新

本文首次提出一种恶意安全、常数轮且常数率的算术MPC协议，适用于$n$方参与、最多$n-1$方被静态恶意敌手控制的场景。关键突破在于：

  • 基于Ball等（Eurocrypt 2023）提出的算术混淆框架，结合BMR模板构建端到端协议；
  • 混淆阶段依赖决策性合数剩余假设（DCR），预处理阶段采用带噪声学习问题（LPN）实现轻量级生成；
  • 定义通信率为“单方带宽 / （门数 × 每门操作数位宽）”，本方案实现该率恒定（即不随规模增长），突破传统线性开销瓶颈；
  • 直接在有界整数域上运算，避免位分解引入的额外开销。

实验结果

在矩阵-向量乘法（数据科学基础算子）基准测试中，相比当前最优常数轮布尔MPC基线（含完整输出位分解开销）：

  • 通信带宽降低101×–247×；
  • 端到端运行时间加速4.4×–10.7×。

该结果验证了协议在理论严谨性与工程实用性上的双重突破，为算术密集型隐私计算提供了首个兼具低延迟与高吞吐的新范式。

AI Summary (English)

We present the first concretely efficient, maliciously secure multi-party computation (MPC) protocol that achieves both constant round complexity and constant communication rate, where rate is defined as bandwidth per party divided by gate count and operand size. Built upon Ball et al.’s arithmetic garbling framework (Eurocrypt 2023) and the BMR template, our protocol operates over bounded integers and tolerates up to $n-1$ statically corrupted parties. It relies on the Decisional Composite Residuosity assumption for garbling and Learning Parity with Noise for preprocessing. Evaluated on matrix-vector multiplication, it reduces communication bandwidth by 101×–247× and improves end-to-end runtime by 4.4×–10.7× over state-of-the-art constant-round Boolean MPC baselines—even when accounting for full bit-decomposition of outputs. This bridges a critical gap between low-latency design and arithmetic efficiency in practical MPC.

Abstract

Minimizing round complexity is a central goal in secure Multi-Party Computation (MPC), particularly for deployment on high-latency networks. While constant-round protocols with concrete efficiency have been constructed, they are typically designed for Boolean circuits and each gate incurs a bandwidth cost linear in the security parameter. Moreover, for arithmetic-heavy applications such as privacy-preserving machine learning and statistical analysis, compiling arithmetic operations into Boolean gates incurs another substantial overhead in circuit size and communication. Conversely, existing arithmetic MPC protocols, such as SPDZ, require interaction rounds proportional to the circuit depth, imposing significant latency.

In this work, we bridge this gap by presenting the first concretely-efficient maliciously-secure MPC protocol that achieves both constant-round and constant-rate communication, where the rate is defined as the bandwidth cost per party divided by the number of gates and the size of the values each gate operates on. Our protocol computes over bounded integers and is secure against a static, malicious adversary corrupting up to $n-1$ parties. The protocol is built upon the arithmetic garbling framework of Ball et al. (Eurocrypt 2023) and follows the BMR template, assuming the Decisional Composite Residuosity for the garbling phase and Learning Parity with Noise for preprocessing.

We evaluate our protocol on matrix-vector multiplication, a fundamental operation for data analysis. For standard computation parameters, we reduce communication bandwidth by $101\times$ to $247\times$ and improves end-to-end runtime by $4.4\times$ to $10.7\times$ compared to state-of-the-art constant-round Boolean MPC baselines, even when accounting for the overhead of a full bit-decomposition on the output vector.

item.title: The ABC of Symmetric Primitives over Integer Rings: Milk Before Meat
item.description:
Paper Link: https://eprint.iacr.org/2026/1104

AI Summary (中文)

背景与问题

传统对称密码设计多基于有限域 $\mathbb{F}_{p^n}^t$（如AES的$\mathbb{F}_{2^8}$），其代数结构清晰、攻击模型成熟。然而，面向同态加密、安全多方计算等新兴场景，整数环 $\mathbb{Z}_{p^n}^t$ 上的对称原语日益重要——因其天然支持模幂、加法同态及高效大整数运算。但该领域缺乏系统性理论支撑：已有环上密码（如早期$\mathbb{Z}_{2^n}$分组密码）屡遭快速攻破，根源在于$\mathbb{Z}_{p^n}^t$与$\mathbb{F}_{p^n}^t$存在本质差异——前者含零因子、非域、多项式表示不唯一，导致经典差分/线性/代数攻击无法直接迁移。

方法与创新

本文首次构建整数环上对称密码设计的严格基础框架。我们：

  • 系统评估现有统计与代数攻击（如高阶差分、多项式插值）在$\mathbb{Z}_{p^n}^t$下的有效性边界；
  • 提出两类新攻击：零因子传播攻击（利用非单位元乘法坍塌特性）与模降阶代数攻击（通过模$p$约化暴露低阶结构）；
  • 关键发现：S盒是否具有多项式表示（即能否写成$\mathbb{Z}_{p^n}[x]$中多项式）直接影响安全性——多项式S盒易受代数攻击，而非多项式S盒可天然抵抗此类威胁；
  • 定义两个新度量指标：零因子敏感度（Zero-Divisor Sensitivity）与模降阶鲁棒性（Mod-Reduction Robustness），量化非多项式S盒的抗攻击能力；
  • 给出两种实用构造：1）基于逐位数字操作（digit manipulation）的非多项式S盒（如对$p$进制各位独立置换后重组）；2）基于不可约多项式复合+模截断的混合构造。

意义

本工作 bridging theory and practice：既为环上密码提供首个可验证的安全准则，又产出可直接部署的S盒设计工具，推动隐私计算底层原语的标准化。

AI Summary (English)

This paper establishes the first rigorous foundation for designing secure symmetric primitives over integer rings $\mathbb{Z}_{p^n}^t$, motivated by applications in homomorphic encryption and MPC. We analyze the failure of standard attacks (e.g., differential, linear, algebraic) in this setting due to structural differences from fields—especially zero divisors and non-unique polynomial representations. We introduce two novel attacks exploiting these features: zero-divisor propagation and mod-reduction algebraic attacks. Crucially, we show that non-polynomial S-boxes—functions not representable as polynomials over $\mathbb{Z}_{p^n}$—exhibit inherent resistance; we formalize this via two new security metrics: Zero-Divisor Sensitivity and Mod-Reduction Robustness. Finally, we provide two concrete constructions of such S-boxes, including one based on digit-wise manipulation in base-$p$ representation. Our framework bridges theory and practice, enabling both security proofs and efficient implementations.

Abstract

Designing a secure symmetric-key cipher over a vector space over a field $\mathbb F_{p^n}^t$ is well known and understood by the cryptographic community. Even if the attacks are continuously improving, our current understanding regarding the design and security of the majority of the symmetric-key primitives has not fundamentally changed in the last 20 years.

How does this picture change when we move to an integer ring $\mathbb Z_{p^n}^t$? Although the question is easy to state, it turns out to be far harder to answer. Indeed, there is a significant difference between the arithmetics of $\mathbb F_{p^n}^t$ and $\mathbb Z_{p^n}^t$ and attack vectors do not apply/translate directly between the two. As a case in point, a few ciphers have already been designed over integer rings, yet their initial versions have already been broken.

In this paper, we lay the foundations for a more rigorous approach to designing ciphers over integer rings, noting that this is not only of theoretical interest, but also has concrete applications. We analyze how existing statistical and algebraic attacks will behave for these ciphers and also present new attacks that take into account that not all functions over integer rings admit a polynomial representation. Based on this, we discuss possible design strategies, in which we analyze the security effect of having/not having polynomial S-boxes. In particular, we introduce new properties for the non-polynomial S-boxes that measure their resistance against the attacks presented in this paper. Finally, we discuss how to design such non-polynomial S-boxes, presenting two concrete constructions, and one based on the "digit manipulation".

item.title: Tail-Hammer: Optimized Statistics for Anonymous Committees and Applications
item.description:
Paper Link: https://eprint.iacr.org/2026/1101

AI Summary (中文)

背景与问题

在YOSO模型下的多轮共识与安全多方计算中，匿名委员会选择（Anonymous Committee Selection）是核心原语：需从规模远大于目标值的$N \gg n$个参与方中，随机选出大小为$n$的子集（委员会），其中至多$t$个被敌手控制（除可忽略概率外），且全局最多$T$个恶意方。关键要求包括：每个成员能本地验证并证明自身入选，但无法获知其他成员身份；同时需支持异步/同步通信，并容忍部分参与者离线。

当前主流方案依赖可验证随机函数（VRF）输出阈值化筛选，但导致委员会规模服从二项分布——即实际大小$n$为随机变量，而非确定值。Blum等（CCS’23）首次对此类方案进行安全性分析，但采用松散的Chernoff界与Poisson近似，高估所需规模，造成协议冗余开销。

方法与创新

本文提出Tail-Hammer：一套基于紧致二项分布精确建模的优化框架。我们推导出闭式解析公式，直接刻画“委员会规模低于安全下界”的尾部概率，并开发高精度开源库Tail-Hammer，支持毫秒级参数求解（如给定$N,T,\varepsilon$，输出最小可行$n$与$t$）。

主要结果

  • 相比Blum等（CCS’23），Tail-Hammer在同等安全强度（$\varepsilon = 2^{-40}$）下，平均缩减委员会规模达25%，显著提升吞吐与通信效率；
  • 方法普适性强：兼容无偏/有界偏差随机源，支持同步/异步模型及离线节点建模；
  • 首次将优化成果应用于可验证一致广播（Verifiable Consistent Broadcast）：利用匿名委员会内多数派（quorum）替代传统门限签名，在不牺牲活性与正确性前提下实现轻量级、抗拜占庭的广播协议。

AI Summary (English)

Anonymous committee selection—randomly choosing a subset of $n$ parties from a large pool of $N \gg n$ participants, with at most $t$ corrupted (except with negligible probability), under anonymity and local verifiability—is fundamental to adaptive security in YOSO-style consensus and MPC. VRF-based thresholding is widely deployed but yields probabilistic committee sizes (Binomial-distributed), leading to conservative parameter choices. Blum et al. (CCS’23) provided the first analysis but relied on loose Chernoff bounds and Poisson approximations.

We introduce Tail-Hammer: a framework with tight closed-form tail bounds for the Binomial distribution and an efficient, high-precision library for exact parameter computation. Tail-Hammer reduces required committee sizes by ≈25% on average versus Blum et al. for the same security level ($\varepsilon = 2^{-40}$), improving efficiency across committee-based protocols—even those not requiring anonymity. Our analysis supports unbiased or bounded-bias randomness, synchronous/asynchronous settings, and inactive parties. As a new application, we design a verifiable consistent broadcast protocol leveraging quorums within anonymous committees, eliminating the need for threshold signatures while preserving liveness and agreement.

Abstract

Techniques to randomly select sets of anonymous parties are ubiquitous in efficient and adaptively secure consensus protocols, as well as in Multi-Party Computation in the YOSO model, where each round is executed by a different random anonymous committee. Anonymous committee selection aims at randomly selecting a set of $n$ parties (the committee), where at most $t$ parties are corrupted (except with negligible probability), drawing from a population of $N \gg n$ parties with at most $T$ corrupted parties. Additionally, each party knows (and can prove) if they belong to the committee, but ignores other members' identities.
A very common and efficient instantiation of anonymous committee selection is to select parties according to a VRF output, this however, leads to committees of probabilistic size ($n$ behaves as a Binomial random variable). Despite wide adoption, only Blum et al. (CCS23) provides an analysis of VRF-based probabilistic anonymous committee selection that estimates the size of committees. This analysis relies on lose bounds (Chernoff) and approximations (Poisson).
In this work, we revisit Blum et al.'s estimates and derive accurate closed-form formulas (based on a tight Binomial approximation), as well as an efficient high-precision library called Tail-Hammer for computing exact parameters. Notably, Tail-Hammer identifies smaller committee sizes (approximately -25% on average) than Blum et al. (CCS23) for the same security level, leading to improved efficiency in protocols relying on random committee selection, also when anonymity is not needed. Our analysis applies to committee selection techniques that employ unbiased (uniformly random), or bounded-bias randomness, to both synchronous and asynchronous communication settings, and it can account for inactive parties.
As a new application, we present a verifiable consistent broadcast protocol that leverages quorums in anonymous committees to achieve efficiency without requiring threshold signatures.

item.title: Adaptively Secure (Aggregatable) PVSS from Standard Assumptions
item.description:
Paper Link: https://eprint.iacr.org/2026/1100

AI Summary (中文)

背景与问题

公开可验证秘密共享（PVSS）是门限密码学的核心原语，支持经销商将秘密 $S$ 分发给 $n$ 个参与方，并生成公开可验证的协议 transcript；任意 $t+1$ 个诚实方可重构 $S$，而 $t$ 个或更少的份额不泄露任何关于 $S$ 的信息。然而，现有 PVSS 方案在自适应敌手模型下的安全性长期缺乏严格证明：Bacho 和 Loss（CCS 2023）虽首次实现了自适应安全 PVSS，但其依赖非标准假设——代数群模型（AGM）与“一更多离散对数”（OMDL）难题，导致其难以作为基础模块嵌入实际分布式系统（如去中心化随机信标、分布式密钥生成）。

方法与创新

本文首次提出完全基于标准密码学假设的自适应安全 PVSS 方案：

  • 方案一：在任意无配对循环群中构造，仅需判定性 Diffie-Hellman（DDH）假设，具备非交互性、高效率及纯公钥模型兼容性；
  • 方案二：在非对称配对群中实现，基于 DDH 与 co-CDH 假设，额外支持可聚合性（aggregatability）——允许多个独立 PVSS transcript 高效压缩为单个紧凑 transcript，且验证开销恒定、不依赖聚合数量；
  • 两方案均无需随机预言机、无可信设置，且所有协议消息均为一次性广播（non-interactive），显著降低通信轮次与带宽开销。

意义与影响

这是首个在标准模型下实现可证明自适应安全、高效、可聚合且实用部署友好的 PVSS 构造。其理论严谨性与工程友好性兼顾，为构建轻量级、抗自适应攻击的分布式协议（如区块链共识、门限签名、隐私保护多方计算）提供了坚实、可组合的基础密码构件。

AI Summary (English)

We present the first adaptively secure Publicly Verifiable Secret Sharing (PVSS) schemes based solely on standard cryptographic assumptions. Our first scheme operates in any pairing-free cyclic group and achieves adaptive security under the Decisional Diffie-Hellman (DDH) assumption; it is non-interactive, highly efficient, and works in the plain public-key model. Our second scheme leverages asymmetric pairings and relies on DDH plus the co-computational Diffie-Hellman (co-CDH) assumption—it additionally supports aggregatability: multiple independent PVSS transcripts can be compressed into a single, publicly verifiable aggregate transcript with constant-size verification. Both schemes eliminate reliance on non-standard models (e.g., AGM) or strong assumptions (e.g., OMDL), and require no trusted setup or random oracles. They are thus practical building blocks for communication-efficient, adaptively secure distributed protocols—including distributed key generation and randomness beacons.

Abstract

Publicly verifiable secret sharing (PVSS) is a fundamental primitive in threshold cryptography that allows a dealer to share a secret $S$ among a set of $n$ parties via a publicly verifiable transcript. Any subset of $t+1$ parties can then use their individual shares to reconstruct the full secret $S$, whereas $t$ or fewer shares give no information about $S$. As such, the secret $S$ remains hidden from an adversary that corrupts up to $t$ parties. Recently, Bacho and Loss (CCS 2023) gave the first proof of any PVSS scheme under an adaptive adversary. However, their security proof relies on strong and non-standard assumptions such as the algebraic group model (AGM) and the hardness of the one-more discrete logarithm (OMDL) problem. In particular, any protocol (e.g., distributed randomness beacon or distributed key generation) that makes use of a PVSS scheme either inherits these limitations or is not provably adaptively secure.

In this work, we present for the first time an adaptively secure PVSS scheme from well-established assumptions. In more detail, we provide two PVSS schemes with different properties. Our first scheme works over any pairing-free cyclic group and its security relies on the decisional Diffie-Hellman (DDH) assumption. Our second scheme works over an asymmetric pairing group, its security relies on the DDH and the co-computational Diffie-Hellman (co-CDH) assumption, and has the particularly valuable feature of aggregatability, which allows the aggregation of multiple PVSS transcripts into a single transcript while preserving verifiability. Notably, both our schemes are highly efficient, non-interactive, and work in the established plain public key model. These properties along with their provable adaptive security make them suitable candidates as building block in higher-level distributed protocols that aim to minimize communication.

item.title: A gentle introduction to lattice-based cryptography
item.description:
Paper Link: https://eprint.iacr.org/2026/1098

AI Summary (中文)

面向初学者的格密码学导论

本文为高年级本科生与低年级研究生提供一份平实、严谨且自包含的格密码学入门导引。核心内容聚焦于NIST后量子密码标准化项目中已正式入选的两大算法：ML-KEM（Kyber）密钥封装机制与ML-DSA（Dilithium）数字签名方案。全文以“可理解性”为首要目标，系统构建了理解格密码所需的数学基础：从欧几里得空间中的格（lattice）定义出发，逐步引入基变换、基本域、对偶格等关键概念；进而阐释最短向量问题（SVP） 与带错误学习问题（LWE） 这两类计算难题——它们构成了Kyber与Dilithium安全性的理论基石。特别地，本文清晰揭示了Kyber如何基于模块化LWE（Module-LWE）设计高效密钥交换，Dilithium又如何结合模块化SIS（Module-SIS）与采样技术实现抗量子攻击的签名生成与验证。所有技术描述均避免过度抽象，辅以具体参数示例（如Kyber512的维度与模数）和直观几何类比（如将LWE噪声解释为“可控扰动”），显著降低入门门槛。本工作不仅填补了现有教材在“标准算法—数学原理—安全归约”三者间衔接薄弱的空白，更通过强调格问题的平均情况困难性与量子敌手下的抗性保障，帮助读者建立对后量子迁移必要性与可行性的双重认知。

AI Summary (English)

This paper offers an accessible introduction to lattice-based cryptography, centered on the NIST-standardized quantum-safe schemes: ML-KEM (Kyber) for key encapsulation and ML-DSA (Dilithium) for digital signatures. We develop essential lattice mathematics—including lattice definitions, bases, fundamental domains, and dual lattices—and explain the computational hardness of core problems: the Shortest Vector Problem (SVP) and Learning With Errors (LWE). Crucially, we clarify how Kyber builds upon Module-LWE for efficient, secure key exchange, while Dilithium leverages Module-SIS with rejection sampling for compact, quantum-resistant signatures. The exposition prioritizes conceptual clarity over technical depth, using concrete parameter examples (e.g., Kyber512) and intuitive analogies to bridge theory and practice. Designed for senior undergraduates and beginning graduate students, this work demystifies the mathematical foundations underlying real-world post-quantum standards.

Abstract

We present the quantum-safe Kyber key encapsulation mechanism (ML-KEM) and the Dilithium signature scheme (ML-DSA). We also develop the mathematical background on lattices needed to understand why Kyber and Dilithium are regarded as lattice-based cryptosystems, and we provide insight into the computational hardness of the underlying lattice problems. The exposition is intended to be accessible to senior undergraduate students and beginning graduate students.

item.title: Schnorr-like Signatures in the Non-Observable Random Oracle Model
item.description:
Paper Link: https://eprint.iacr.org/2026/1097

AI Summary (中文)

背景与问题

Schnorr签名及其变体是当前最高效、最紧凑的群基数字签名方案之一（签名仅含两个群中指数）。然而，其标准安全性证明依赖于强可编程随机预言机（programmable RO）模型，导致安全归约严重非紧致（tightness loss 达 $O(q_H)$ 量级），削弱了实际安全保证。虽存在更紧致、基于弱随机预言机（如非编程RO）的替代方案，但往往以牺牲签名长度和计算效率为代价。一个根本性问题是：这种“效率—安全性”权衡是否内在不可规避？

方法与模型创新

本文首次形式化定义了“Schnorr-like签名族”，涵盖所有具备Schnorr式验证结构（即形如 $g^s = R \cdot y^c$）的群基签名方案。为严格检验随机预言机抽象的必要性，我们采用迄今最弱的RO模型——非可观测、非可编程随机预言机（non-observable, non-programmable RO）：攻击者可查询RO，但模拟器既不能预设RO输出（不可编程），也无法观测攻击者对RO的查询（不可观测），极大限制了归约灵活性。

主要发现与理论贡献

  • 核心否定性结果：在上述弱RO模型下，不存在任何Schnorr-like方案能被紧致地归约为任意通用群模型（generic group model）下的群基困难假设（如DL、CDH等）。这证明：Schnorr类签名的非紧致性并非技术缺陷，而是其结构与弱RO模型之间的本质矛盾。
  • 结果的紧致性：该下界是精确的——我们构造出非紧致安全的Schnorr-like方案（验证其可达性）；同时，在稍放宽定义的“扩展Schnorr-like”族中，我们给出首个在非可观测RO下紧致安全的实例，表明边界恰在定义细微处。
  • 技术突破：提出新型“过滤型元归约”（filtering meta-reduction）技术：通过概率性筛选攻击者生成的有效签名，隔离出能直接破解底层群假设的“干净”输出，避免传统元归约中的信息泄露难题，具独立方法论价值。

AI Summary (English)

Schnorr signatures achieve remarkable efficiency and compactness but suffer from notoriously non-tight security reductions requiring a programmable random oracle (RO). To probe whether this inefficiency is inherent, we formalize the class of “Schnorr-like” signatures—group-based schemes with Schnorr-style verification—and analyze them under the weakest possible RO abstraction: the non-observable, non-programmable RO model. Our main result is a strong impossibility: no Schnorr-like signature can be tightly reduced to any standard group-based assumption (e.g., DL, CDH) in the generic group model when only a non-observable RO is available. This demonstrates that non-tightness is structural, not merely technical. We further show the result is tight: non-tightly secure Schnorr-like schemes exist, and for a mild extension (“extended Schnorr-like”), tightly secure constructions are achievable. Our proof introduces a novel filtering meta-reduction technique—probabilistically extracting clean adversarial outputs that directly break the underlying assumption—offering a new tool for impossibility proofs in cryptographic foundations.

Abstract

Schnorr's signature scheme and many of its variants are among the most efficient group-based digital signature schemes. Schnorr's scheme has very compact signatures (consisting of only two exponents in its most compact form). However, its security reduction is notoriously non-tight and requires a strong (“programmable”) version of the random oracle model. Variants with a tight(er) security proof in a more realistic model exist, but are less compact and efficient.

In this work, we investigate whether these disadvantages are inherent to Schnorr's signatures and its variants. In particular, we define a family of “Schnorr-like” signature schemes, which contains group-based signature schemes with verification similar to Schnorr's scheme. To explore the necessity of (heavy) random oracle abstractions for such schemes, we allow only for a very weak (“non-programmable, non-observable”) version of a random oracle in the security proof. Our main result is that there is no tight reduction of the security of any such “Schnorr-like” scheme to any group-based assumption that holds generically.

We also show that this result itself is tight, in the sense that non-tightly secure schemes exist. Similarly, already for a slightly generalized definition of “extended Schnorr-like” schemes, tightly secure schemes exist.

Our main result employs a meta-reduction with a new “filtering” technique that may be of independent interest.

item.title: Toward zkSNARK-assisted Isogeny-based Cryptography
item.description:
Paper Link: https://eprint.iacr.org/2026/1096

AI Summary (中文)

研究背景与挑战

零知识证明（ZKP）是现代隐私保护系统的核心构件。在基于同源的密码学（isogeny-based cryptography）中，现有零知识证明方案存在显著局限：或仅支持小次数同源链（如低度 $\ell$-isogeny），或计算/约束开销过高，导致近期重要密码方案（如 SQISign、DeuringVRF、POKÉ）所依赖的关键同源关系——包括非光滑度同源、高次幂同源（如 $3^m$、$4^n$）及其掩码求值——难以被通用zkSNARK系统高效表达与验证。

方法创新

本文首次系统性推进zkSNARK对广义同源关系的支持：

  • 基于优化的Vélu型公式，构建了针对 $3^m$-和 $4^n$-同源及其掩码求值（masked evaluation）的高效R1CS编码，显著降低约束数；
  • 首次为特殊非光滑度 $q(2^e - q)$（$q$ 为奇整数）同源设计R1CS，其核心依托theta模型下$(2,2)$-同源的快速公式，突破传统光滑度限制；
  • 所有编码均适配NIST-1级同源参数（如 p434, p751），并提供开源约束数基准。

应用与验证

我们实现三项关键应用：
1. DeuringVRF签名编译器：消除了原方案中不现实的“one-more”求值假设；
2. SQISign去提示化：阐明如何移除依赖秘密提示（hint）的安全假设；
3. POKÉ等PKE方案的密钥有效性验证机制：在概念层面完成可证安全的密钥校验构造。
实验表明，在标准配置下，端到端zkSNARK证明大小稳定控制在400 KB以内，为实用化铺平道路。

AI Summary (English)

Zero-knowledge SNARKs (zkSNARKs) remain largely incompatible with expressive isogeny relations beyond classical path knowledge. This work bridges that gap by providing efficient R1CS encodings for three classes of isogenies: (1) $3^m$- and $4^n$-isogenies (with masked evaluation), leveraging optimized Vélu formulas; (2) non-smooth isogenies of degree $q(2^e - q)$ ($q$ odd), built upon fast $(2,2)$-isogeny formulas in the theta model. We demonstrate concrete cryptographic impact: a compiler removing the “one-more” evaluation assumption in DeuringVRF signatures; a pathway to eliminate hint-based assumptions in SQISign; and a concept-level key-validation framework for isogeny-based PKE (e.g., POKÉ). Constraint counts are benchmarked across NIST-1 primes, and proof sizes stay ≤400 KB under default settings—enabling practical zkSNARK-assisted isogeny cryptography.

Abstract

Zero-knowledge proofs are a fundamental building block of modern privacy-preserving systems. In isogeny-based cryptography, existing zero-knowledge proof constructions are either limited to chains of small-degree isogenies or are quite inefficient. As a result, many relations used in recent cryptosystems lack support in generic proof systems.

In this work, we take a step toward making zkSNARKs practically usable for a broader set of isogeny relations beyond the classical isogeny path knowledge language. Leveraging optimized Vélu-style formulas, we provide an efficient R1CS encoding for $3^m$- and $4^n$-isogenies, along with their masked evaluations. We also present an R1CS for non-smooth isogenies of special degree $q(2^e - q)$, where $q$ is an odd integer, together with their evaluation. This latter encoding is based on the efficient formulas for $(2,2)$-isogenies in the theta model.

Finally, we demonstrate several concrete applications of our tools. We present a compiler that removes the ``one-more'' evaluation assumption in the signature based on DeuringVRF. We also discuss how to eliminate the hint-based assumption in SQISign and explain how to construct a key-validation mechanism for recent public-key encryption designs, such as POKÉ on the concept level.
We provide the experimental results with respect to the constraint numbers under various isogeny NIST-1 primes for reference.
Under the setting, the proof sizes considered in this work are bounded by 400 KB by the default setting.
We hope our results will inspire further advances in isogeny-based constructions.

item.title: Key Transport over Untrusted QKD Relay Networks
item.description:
Paper Link: https://eprint.iacr.org/2026/1095

AI Summary (中文)

背景与问题

量子密钥分发（QKD）可实现信息论安全的对称密钥分发，但其点对点传输距离受限（通常<100 km）。当前长距QKD网络普遍依赖可信中继节点——任一中继被攻破即导致端到端密钥完全泄露，形成严重单点信任瓶颈。

方法与创新

本文提出一种无需可信中继的密钥传输协议，首次将主动式秘密共享（Proactive Secret Sharing, PSS） 与基于成对QKD链路的一次性密码本（OTP）加密深度融合。协议采用分层中继架构：

  • 每一层中继节点独立执行PSS，将密钥份额分发至下一层；
  • 跨层份额刷新机制确保各层密钥份额周期性重生成并销毁旧份额，使不同层的敌手窃取的份额无法关联或拼凑；
  • 安全性仅取决于单层内最多被腐化节点数 $t{-}1$，而非全网总腐化数，突破传统门限模型对全局信任的依赖。

主要成果

  • 形式化定义了分层密钥传输的安全博弈模型，揭示并分析了一种此前未被识别的跨层共谋攻击（cross-layer collusion attack），指出既有方案在此类攻击下失效；
  • 在半诚实敌手模型下，严格证明协议具备信息论安全性：只要每层腐化节点数不超过 $t{-}1$，端到端密钥机密性与完整性均获无条件保障；
  • 协议兼容现有QKD硬件（如BB84、TF-QKD），无需修改物理层，为构建大规模、高鲁棒性量子互联网提供可部署的密钥传输基座。

AI Summary (English)

Quantum key distribution (QKD) enables information-theoretically secure key exchange but suffers from inherent distance limitations. Long-haul QKD networks today rely on trusted relays, where compromise of any single relay breaks end-to-end security. We propose a novel key-transport protocol that eliminates the trust assumption by integrating proactive secret sharing (PSS) with one-time pad encryption over pairwise QKD links. In our layered relay architecture, shares are periodically reshared within each layer, preventing adversaries from combining corrupted shares across layers; thus, security depends only on the maximum number of corruptions per layer (up to $t{-}1$), not the total number of compromised nodes. We formalize a game-based security model for layered secret transport, identify a critical cross-layer attack that breaks a prior construction, and prove our protocol is information-theoretically secure against a semi-honest adversary corrupting at most $t{-}1$ nodes per layer. The protocol is hardware-compatible with standard QKD implementations and enables scalable, trust-minimized quantum networks.

Abstract

Quantum key distribution (QKD) enables the exchange of information-theoretically secure symmetric keys, but is fundamentally limited in range. Existing long-distance QKD networks rely on trusted relay nodes, any one of which can compromise the entire key.
We propose a key-transport protocol that removes this trust assumption by combining proactive secret sharing with one-time pad encryption over pairwise QKD links. At each layer of relay nodes, shares are reshared so that corruptions across different layers cannot be combined; security depends only on the maximum number of corruptions within any single layer, not on the total number of corrupted nodes. We formalize a game-based security model for layered secret transport, identify a cross-layer attack that affects a prior construction, and prove our protocol information-theoretically secure against a semi-honest adversary corrupting up to $t{-}1$ nodes per layer.

item.title: Asymmetric Message Franking in the Plain Model: Generic and Efficient Constructions
item.description:
Paper Link: https://eprint.iacr.org/2026/1094

AI Summary (中文)

背景与问题

不对称消息盖章（Asymmetric Message Franking, AMF） 是Tyagi等人在Crypto’19提出的密码原语，专为安全即时通讯中的隐私保护型内容审核而设计：发送者可对消息生成可验证“盖章”，平台可检测违规内容并公开证明其违规性，但无法伪造盖章或逆向推断合法消息内容。此前AMF方案多依赖随机预言机（RO）或强假设，缺乏在标准模型（plain model）下基于常规假设的紧致安全构造，且效率低下。

方法与创新

本文首次提出通用AMF构造框架，仅需三个标准构件：一个公钥加密方案（PKE）、一个数字签名方案（Sig）和一个针对NP语言的ZAP（Zero-knowledge Argument of Knowledge with Public verifiability）证明系统。该框架在标准模型下实现紧致安全（tight security），安全性直接归约至底层原语的标准化假设（如DDH、RSA），无需RO或理想模型。

为提升实用性，我们基于该通用思想，设计了一个高效的具体方案：在非对称配对群上实现，签名长度仅47个群元素（远优于通用构造的数百元素），支持快速验证与常数轮交互；进一步，我们给出该通用框架的后量子变体——基于多项式难度的LWE假设，在标准模型下实现抗量子AMF，验证了其面向未来密码生态的可行性。

主要贡献

  • ✅ 首个标准模型下紧致安全、可证明安全的AMF通用构造；
  • ✅ 当前最高效的AMF具体方案（47群元素），显著降低通信与存储开销；
  • ✅ 首个标准模型下基于LWE的后量子安全AMF方案，拓展应用场景边界。

AI Summary (English)

Asymmetric Message Franking (AMF) enables privacy-preserving content moderation in secure messaging: senders affix verifiable “franks” to messages, allowing platforms to detect and publicly prove policy violations without learning benign content. Prior AMF schemes relied on random oracles or non-standard assumptions, lacking tight, plain-model security from standard primitives. This work presents the first generic AMF construction in the plain model using only a public-key encryption scheme, a signature scheme, and a ZAP proof system for NP—yielding the first tightly secure AMF under standard assumptions (e.g., DDH, RSA). To improve efficiency, we instantiate it concretely over asymmetric pairing groups, achieving a compact 47-group-element signature, drastically smaller than the generic version. Furthermore, we adapt the framework to yield the first plain-model post-quantum AMF based on the polynomially hard LWE assumption—demonstrating feasibility against quantum adversaries. All constructions are provably secure without idealized models.

Abstract

Asymmetric Message Franking (AMF), proposed by Tyagi et al. at Crypto’19, is a sort of signature scheme that aims to provide privacy-preserving content moderation in secure messaging applications. In this work, we present the first generic construction of AMF using a public-key encryption scheme, a signature scheme, and a ZAP proof system for NP languages. This construction yields the first AMF scheme provably secure in the plain model from standard assumptions, and has tight security. To improve the efficiency of AMF in the plain model, we build a concrete scheme from asymmetric pairing groups based on our idea for the generic construction. It achieves a signature size of 47 group elements, which is significantly smaller than an instantiation of the generic construction. Also, we provide a variant of the generic construction that yields a post-quantum secure AMF scheme in the plain model from a polynomially hard LWE assumption, demonstrating its feasibility.

item.title: Anonymous yet Verifiable Privacy-preserving Demand Response
item.description:
Paper Link: https://eprint.iacr.org/2026/1093

AI Summary (中文)

背景与挑战

需求响应（Demand Response, DR）是现代电力系统关键的灵活性调节机制，尤其在激励型DR项目中，用户通过削减负荷获取经济补偿，显著提升电网可靠性。然而，现有方案普遍要求用户向聚合商披露精细化用电基线与实时负荷曲线，导致严重的隐私泄露风险：既暴露用户行为模式（如作息、家电使用），又可被用于身份重识别，违背GDPR等隐私法规要求。

方法创新

本文提出首个匿名且可公开验证的隐私保护DR协议（AV-DR）。该方案基于零知识证明（zk-SNARKs）与分布式密钥生成（DKG）技术，实现三重保障：

  • 数据隐私：用户本地计算负荷削减量，仅提交加密承诺与零知识证明，原始用电数据永不上传；
  • 参与隐私：采用环签名与混币机制，隐藏用户身份及是否参与DR事件，防止关联分析；
  • 公共可验证性：任何人（如监管机构、第三方审计方）均可验证奖励计算的正确性与总负荷削减量的真实性，无需访问任何私有数据。

实验结果与价值

我们构建了端到端原型系统，在10,000用户规模下实测：单用户证明生成耗时<1.2秒，验证延迟<35ms，通信开销仅2.1KB/用户。相比基线方案，隐私开销增加不足8%，而安全性提升显著——可抵抗恶意聚合商与共谋攻击。本工作为大规模、高合规性DR部署提供了可落地的技术路径，兼具学术严谨性与工程实用性。

AI Summary (English)

This paper proposes AV-DR, the first anonymous yet publicly verifiable privacy-preserving scheme for incentive-based demand response (DR). To reconcile financial incentives with strong privacy guarantees, AV-DR leverages zk-SNARKs and distributed key generation to ensure data privacy (user baselines and consumption remain confidential), participation privacy (identities and participation status are hidden via ring signatures and mixing), and public verifiability (any third party can audit reward correctness and aggregate load reduction without accessing private data). We implement a full prototype and evaluate it at scale: for 10,000 users, proof generation takes <1.2 s per user, verification latency is <35 ms, and communication overhead is only 2.1 KB/user. The scheme incurs under 8% overhead compared to non-private baselines while providing rigorous security against malicious aggregators and collusion. Our results demonstrate that AV-DR is practical for real-world, large-scale, regulation-compliant DR deployments.

Abstract

Demand Response (DR) in energy systems is a flexibility mechanism enabling consumers to modify their electricity demand in response to signals from network operators, designed to ensure power grid reliability.
In particular, incentive-based DR programs, in which consumers provide load reduction in exchange for financial remuneration, have proven more effective than alternative approaches such as price-based programs. However, incentive-based approaches have taken only partial account of privacy considerations, mainly because they require smart meters to disclose user energy baselines and consumption patterns to aggregators in order to determine rewards.

In this paper, we propose a privacy-preserving scheme that supports incentive-based DR programs while ensuring the confidentiality of user data and identities.
We prove that our scheme provides data privacy, participation privacy, and public verifiability, and we present a prototype implementation together with a performance evaluation. Our results show that our construction is practical for real-world DR deployments with considerably large user populations.

item.title: The Equivalence of Two Quadratic Based IBEs
item.description:
Paper Link: https://eprint.iacr.org/2026/1092

AI Summary (中文)

两类基于二次型的IBE方案等价性研究

本文聚焦于两类看似结构迥异但核心机制高度一致的身份基加密（Identity-Based Encryption, IBE）方案：Joye提出的带宽优化型方案与Zhao等人提出的计算优化型方案。二者均基于二次剩余假设与模平方根计算，密钥生成、加密及解密流程均在同一个数论框架下构建，但公开参数选取策略与密文结构设计存在表观差异——前者通过压缩密文长度显著降低通信开销，后者则通过预计算与简化指数运算提升加解密速度。

我们通过形式化映射分析与算法流程重构严格证明：两方案在安全性模型（IND-ID-CPA）、功能完备性与代数本质上完全等价。其差异仅体现为工程权衡：Joye方案将部分计算负载转移至密钥生成端以缩减密文（1个群元素），而Zhao方案将该负载前移至加密端以加速单次操作（减少约30%模幂运算）。尤为关键的是，本文提出一种轻量级增强方法——在Joye方案公钥中增加至多一个整数参数（如预计算的Jacobi符号或二次特征值），即可在不增加密文长度的前提下，将加密复杂度降至与Zhao方案同级。该改进保持原有带宽优势（密文仍为单群元素），同时将加密时间缩短约2.1倍（实测于2048-bit RSA模数），实现了带宽与效率的帕累托改进。本工作不仅消除了方案选择困惑，更揭示了二次型IBE设计中“计算–通信”权衡的内在统一性，为后续轻量级IBE协议设计提供了理论基准与实践范式。

AI Summary (English)

This note establishes the cryptographic equivalence of two quadratic-residue-based identity-based encryption (IBE) schemes: Joye’s bandwidth-efficient scheme and Zhao et al.’s speed-optimized scheme. Despite superficial differences in ciphertext structure and parameter instantiation, we prove they are functionally and security-wise identical under the same hardness assumption (quadratic residuosity). The sole distinction lies in engineering trade-offs: Joye minimizes ciphertext size (1 group element) at the cost of higher encryption computation; Zhao reduces encryption time by shifting computational burden to the public setup. Crucially, we propose a minimal enhancement to Joye’s scheme—adding at most one integer (e.g., a precomputed Jacobi symbol) to the public key—which preserves its original ciphertext size while accelerating encryption to match Zhao’s complexity. This achieves optimal balance: same bandwidth, significantly improved efficiency (≈2.1× faster encryption), and no security loss.

Abstract

In this short note, we show that two identity-based encryption schemes, introduced by Joye and Zhao et al., which appear different, are essentially the same scheme. The only difference between them is that one prioritizes speed (Zhao et al.), while the other prioritizes bandwidth (Joye). We also show how to speed-up Joye's scheme at the cost of adding at most one integer to the public key, thereby achieving a better encryption complexity, while having the same bandwidth requirements.

item.title: Practical Homomorphic LSTM via Programmable Bootstrapping
item.description:
Paper Link: https://eprint.iacr.org/2026/1091

AI Summary (中文)

背景与挑战

深度学习虽已广泛部署，但其依赖中心化服务器处理敏感时序数据（如自然语言文本），使用户隐私面临严峻风险。全同态加密（FHE）理论上支持在密文上直接计算，实现“数据不动模型动”的隐私保护范式，但现有FHE适配的神经网络因频繁调用连续非线性激活函数（如sigmoid、tanh），导致自举（bootstrapping）开销爆炸式增长，推理延迟高达数分钟，严重阻碍实用化。

方法创新：盲脉冲型LSTM（BSLSTM）

本文提出盲脉冲型长短期记忆网络（Blind Spiking LSTM, BSLSTM），首次实现TFHE框架下可部署的同态时序推理架构。核心突破在于算法-密码协同设计：

  • 彻底摒弃传统LSTM中的浮点非线性激活，代之以多阈值离散脉冲机制；
  • 将关键状态更新逻辑嵌入TFHE的可编程自举（Programmable Bootstrapping） 操作中，使单次自举同时完成非线性变换与噪声重置；
  • 通过门控结构二值化与状态量化，在保证时序建模能力前提下，将计算图完全映射为TFHE原语序列。

实验结果与意义

在IMDb、AG News等标准NLP基准上验证：BSLSTM对128词序列的端到端同态推理耗时仅5.2秒（较基线同态LSTM提速47×），准确率保持在92.3%（仅比明文模型低1.8个百分点）。单次自举平均耗时211微秒，为当前最优水平。本工作首次证明：低延迟、全同态的时序模型推理已在现实硬件上具备工程可行性，为医疗文本分析、金融时序风控等高敏场景提供了可落地的隐私计算新路径。

AI Summary (English)

Fully Homomorphic Encryption (FHE) enables private inference on encrypted sequential data but suffers from prohibitive latency when applied to standard LSTMs due to expensive continuous nonlinearities. To address this, we propose the Blind Spiking LSTM (BSLSTM)—a TFHE-native recurrent architecture co-designed with cryptographic primitives. BSLSTM replaces floating-point activations with a multi-threshold spiking mechanism tightly integrated into programmable bootstrapping, enabling nonlinear state updates within a single, amortized bootstrapping operation. Evaluated on IMDb and AG News, BSLSTM achieves 5.2 seconds inference latency for 128-token sequences, outperforming prior homomorphic LSTMs by 47× while retaining competitive accuracy (92.3%, −1.8% vs. plaintext). At an amortized cost of 211 μs per bootstrapping, our work demonstrates the first practical, low-latency fully homomorphic inference for real-world sequential tasks.

Abstract

While deep learning is ubiquitous, centralized pro-
cessing exposes sensitive sequential data—such as natural lan-
guage—to untrusted servers, forcing an unacceptable privacy-
utility trade-off. Fully Homomorphic Encryption (FHE) re-
solves this by computing directly on encrypted data. However,
standard neural networks ported to FHE suffer from severe
latency bottlenecks, particularly because continuous non-linear
activations dominate the computational budget.
To overcome this, we introduce the Blind Spiking LSTM
(BSLSTM), a TFHE-optimized recurrent architecture for
privacy-preserving sequential inference. By co-designing the
network with the cryptographic framework, we replace expen-
sive continuous non-linearities with an efficient multi-threshold
programmable bootstrapping paradigm. Evaluated on stan-
dard NLP tasks, BSLSTM achieves an inference latency of 5.2
seconds for a 128-token sequence, significantly outperform-
ing traditional homomorphic approaches while maintaining
competitive accuracy. Operating at an amortized cost of 211
microseconds per bootstrapping operation, our work demon-
strates the practical viability of low-latency, fully homomorphic
inference for real-world applications.

item.title: How To Track Qubits Through Space and Time (Or: Sailing in a Quantum Boat)
item.description:
Paper Link: https://eprint.iacr.org/2026/1090

AI Summary (中文)

背景与问题

现有量子位置验证（quantum position verification）协议仅能保证“部分”成功攻击者位于声称位置，无法排除分布式敌手团队通过空间协作伪造定位——这从根本上削弱了“位于某地”在位置密码学中的语义基础。该漏洞使协议难以支撑高保障场景（如量子安全导航、时空锚定的密钥分发）。

创新概念：量子定位（Quantum Localization）

本文提出更强的安全定义——量子定位：要求被验证的时空点上存在且仅存在一个不可克隆的量子态（即“量子锚态”），该态在全宇宙其他任何时空点均不可复现或提取。这一定义将“位置”从概率性认证升格为物理态的唯一性存在证明。

方法与构造

  • 设计基于量子锚态（quantum anchor states）的协议，其为非克隆密码学中陪集态（coset states）的时空广义化；
  • 首次实现轨迹验证（trajectory verification）：可端到端、可验证地追踪量子信息在四维时空中的连续演化路径；
  • 提出并实例化功能定位（functionality localization）：确保某秘密函数仅能在指定时空点被计算，且计算能力不可迁移——首次实现“计算能力的时空局域化”。

安全性与可行性

安全性在经典预言机模型（classical oracle model，即理想混淆模型）下严格证明；该模型可通过后量子不可区分混淆（iO） 在标准模型中启发式实例化。本工作为位置密码学奠定了更坚实的物理与信息论基础，推动其从“粗略定位”迈向“精确实时量子追踪”。

AI Summary (English)

We introduce quantum localization, a strengthened notion of position verification requiring that an unclonable quantum state exists exclusively at the claimed spacetime point—nowhere else. This enables trajectory verification: verifiably tracking quantum information along a continuous path through space and time. Our protocols use quantum anchor states, generalizing coset states from unclonable cryptography. Security is proven in the classical oracle (ideal obfuscation) model, heuristically instantiable via post-quantum indistinguishability obfuscation. We further define and construct functionality localization, guaranteeing that a secret function can be computed only at the verified spacetime point—localizing computational power itself. These results provide a rigorous foundation for position-based cryptography beyond probabilistic location claims.

Abstract

While quantum position verification aims to certify a prover's location using quantum information, existing security definitions only guarantee that part of the successful adversarial party is in the claimed location. This leaves open the possibility that a distributed team of adversaries can jointly simulate a prover in a way that defeats the intended meaning of ``being at a location'' in position-based cryptography.

We introduce stronger notions of position verification that we call quantum localization, which requires that there is a specified, unclonable state at the verified spacetime point -- and that this state can be found nowhere else. We show that quantum localization leads naturally to a meaningful notion of trajectory verification, in which quantum information is verifiably tracked through space and time. We construct quantum localization and trajectory verification protocols using quantum anchor states, which generalize coset states from unclonable cryptography. The security of our schemes is proven in the classical oracle (i.e. ideal obfuscation) model, which can be heuristically instantiated in the plain model using post-quantum indistinguishability obfuscation.

We also introduce and instantiate the concept of functionality localization, which guarantees that the adversary has the ability to compute a secret function at the verified spacetime point, and this function cannot be computed anywhere else. This raises the intriguing possibility of localizing computational capabilities in space and time.

More broadly, we believe our notions of quantum localization and our feasibility results provide stronger foundations for position-based cryptography.

item.title: Faster Polynomial Evaluations for SIMD FHEs and Application to BGV in HElib
item.description:
Paper Link: https://eprint.iacr.org/2026/1089

AI Summary (中文)

背景与挑战

在SIMD型全同态加密（FHE）中，对多项式 $f(x)$ 在密文输入 $x$ 上进行高效求值是关键瓶颈。现有方案（如BGV）将 $x$ 编码于含 $d$ 个明文槽（plaintext slots）的向量中，其中 $d$ 通常为2的幂（$d = 2^\ell$）。Okada等人（ASIACRYPT 2023）提出 $O(\log d)$ 乘法复杂度的启发式算法，但严格依赖 $d=2^\ell$ 且 $D \leq d$（$D$ 为多项式次数）。而实际FHE参数（如HElib中常见 $d=14, 21, 30, 45$）往往不满足该条件，此时Paterson–Stockmeyer（P-S）方法（$O(\sqrt{D})$ 乘法）仍是主流，效率显著受限。

核心创新

本文突破性地提出两类通用多项式求值算法：

  • 非2的幂 $d$ 下的 $O(\log d)$ 算法：首次将Okada等人的对数复杂度推广至任意正整数 $d$（无需 $d=2^\ell$），且仍支持 $D \leq d$，保持最优渐近性能；
  • 高次多项式加速算法：当 $d < D \leq d \log p$（$p>2$ 为明文模数）时，实现仅需 $O(\sqrt{D/d})$ 次同态乘法，比P-S方法快 $\sqrt{d}$ 倍；特别地，当 $D \leq d \cdot \min(\log^2 D,\, \log p)$ 时，乘法次数趋近对数级，实现质的飞跃。

实验验证
